message:
fix(ingest): trim_utf8_tail must respect max_bytes even when last char exceeds it (ft-a0up5)

`trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes` attempts to return the suffix of a
string that fits within `max_bytes` while honouring UTF-8 character
boundaries. The previous fallback, intended to avoid returning an
empty string when the boundary-scan consumed all remaining bytes,
backtracked to include the "last full character" — but that last
character could itself exceed max_bytes.

Concrete failure: `trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes("中", 1)` returned the
full 3-byte `中` even though `max_bytes=1`. Same shape at
`trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes("中", 2)` (returns 3 bytes) and
`trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes("a中", 2)` (returns the 3-byte `中`
suffix). The function silently exceeded the caller's declared cap.

Downstream, `enforce_segment_size_for_persistence` uses this function
to enforce `max_segment_bytes` before persisting segments. The cap
therefore leaked past the call site: a pane whose last character was
wider than the configured max — common with CJK text or emoji —
could persist segments larger than the schema's CHECK ceiling
allows, tripping either the DB constraint or the FTS-column
truncation downstream.

Fix: when the boundary-scan consumes all remaining bytes (`start >=
text.len()`), return `String::new()` rather than backtracking. The
empty string is a sound default because the only persistence-side
caller (`enforce_segment_size_for_persistence`) already wraps the
oversize case in a Gap segment via `truncation_reason` — operators
see the "exceeded max_segment_bytes" signal without the function
secretly exceeding the cap to preserve a character.

In-line doc comment cross-references the caller contract so future
readers understand why empty is correct here rather than a
"last-full-char" fallback.

Pinning tests (under the ft-a0up5 block):

- `ft_a0up5_trim_utf8_tail_returns_at_most_max_bytes_for_multibyte_
   only_text`: asserts result.len() <= max_bytes for both
  trim("中", 1) and trim("中", 2). Pre-fix these returned 3 bytes.
- `ft_a0up5_trim_utf8_tail_handles_2_byte_cap_with_3_byte_trailing
   _char`: trim("a中", 2) — asserts len <= 2 and the returned slice
  is on UTF-8 char boundaries (either "" or "a" is acceptable;
  pre-fix returned the 3-byte 中).
- `ft_a0up5_enforce_segment_size_respects_kept_bytes_le_max_bytes`:
  end-to-end check that the persistence wrapper enforces the cap
  against a real CapturedSegment. Also pins
  `detail.original_bytes=3`, `detail.max_bytes=1`, and
  `detail.kept_bytes <= 1` so the enforcement telemetry reflects
  reality rather than the pre-fix "kept full char" fiction.

No API change; no migration. Callers that depended on the previous
"at least one character" behaviour must now handle the empty case —
but the only such caller is persistence, which already handles it
via the Gap path.
